name: adapter_benchmarks_v1
description: Adapter evaluation and benchmarking dataset for adapterOS. Covers perplexity evaluation, task-specific accuracy assessment, latency benchmarking, regression testing across adapter versions, and evaluation methodology design. All examples reference adapterOS-specific APIs (compute_perplexity, PerplexityImprovement, TrainingConfig) and realistic LoRA parameter ranges (rank 4-32, alpha 8-64).

entries.notes: 2000 examples across 5 subcategories: perplexity_evaluation (400), task_accuracy (400), latency_benchmarks (400), regression_testing (400), evaluation_methodology (400)

provenance.review_notes: Synthetic dataset covering adapter evaluation lifecycle: perplexity assessment, domain-specific accuracy, latency overhead measurement, cross-version regression detection, and evaluation methodology best practices. Numerical examples grounded in adapterOS perplexity module (PPL = exp(loss)) and TrainingConfig parameter ranges.

evaluation_gates: Perplexity values correctly computed as exp(cross_entropy_loss)
Overfitting detection triggers when train perplexity << eval perplexity by factor > 3x
Latency overhead thresholds scale with adapter rank (rank=4 < 2%, rank=32 < 5%)
Regression testing identifies score drops > 2% as statistically significant
Evaluation methodology enforces zero overlap between train and eval sets
Task accuracy metrics are domain-appropriate (F1 for classification, BLEU for generation)
Memory overhead calculations account for both A and B matrices (rank * hidden_dim * 2)
intent: Train the adapter to reason about the complete evaluation lifecycle: assessing trained adapter quality via perplexity, measuring domain-specific task accuracy, benchmarking latency and memory overhead, tracking quality regressions across versions, and designing statistically sound evaluation protocols.
